commit.message:
Replace mem::replace with EmptyWork placeholder with Option<Box<dyn Work>>

Change WorkEntry.work from Box<dyn Work + Send> to Option<Box<dyn Work + Send>>.
Use .take() to move work out during execution and restore with Some(work) after
completion. This makes the 'work is temporarily absent' state explicit and
catchable — None instead of a silently-succeeding placeholder. Delete the
EmptyWork struct and its Work impl.

Closes #1143

commit.message:
Replace RwLock<Option<T>> with OnceLock<T> for set-once fields

Convert envelope_sender, ledger_manager, and upgrades fields in ScpDriver
from RwLock<Option<T>> to OnceLock<T>. These fields are written once during
initialization and read many times on consensus hot paths. OnceLock
eliminates unnecessary locking overhead and makes the set-once semantics
explicit in the type system.

Closes #1141

commit.message:
fix: block openintel only when czds is running same TLD partition

Previously sync_openintel_tld blocked on ANY active CZDS run.
Since the domain table is partitioned by TLD (domain_ac, domain_net, etc.)
there is no actual conflict between openintel/ac and czds/net.

Change has_any_source_running('czds') to has_running_run('czds', tld)
so OpenINTEL is only blocked if CZDS is processing the exact same TLD.

commit.message:
feat: add FORCE_SCALE=400 to make simulation dynamics lively (task 8.2)

Without a force multiplier the raw matrix strengths (max 1.5) produce
F_max = 1.5 / (5²) = 0.06 — far below BOND_THRESHOLD (0.5) and too
weak to drive particles toward their maxSpeed at typical distances.

Added FORCE_SCALE = 400 applied uniformly in applyForces:
  F = strength * FORCE_SCALE / (d_clamped²)

Result:
- A single Lumion-Lumion pair at d=100 now generates a terminal
  velocity of ~1.6 px/frame; multiple neighbors combine to reach maxSpeed.
- Bonds form when strong pairs are within ~35-55 px (e.g. Nullon-Gravon
  threshold distance = sqrt(1.5*400/0.5) ≈ 35 px).
- PHASE and CHAOS interactions now produce visible oscillating/chaotic
  motion rather than noise too small to see.

Also updated 4.5 unit tests:
- Removed the stale 'bonds can never form' comment block.
- Added explicit no-bond case (Lumion-Lumion at d=100, F=0.032 < 0.5).
- Added explicit bond case (Nullon-Gravon at d=5, F=24 >> 0.5).
- Updated invariant check to include FORCE_SCALE in the F formula.
- Fixed stale 4.2 comment that hardcoded the old F value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Adds a .NET 10 minimal API sample that runs a user-provided JavaScript function body in QuickJS compiled to WebAssembly through a separate Node worker process.

- POST /execute accepts a JSON body with a functionBody string
- The API wraps the body in a function accepting a user object with roles: ['User']
- Execution happens in a spawned Node process hosting QuickJS-in-WASM (quickjs-emscripten)
- The function result must be a boolean; anything else returns a 400 error
- A 5-second timeout is enforced both inside QuickJS (interrupt handler) and from the API (process kill)

commit.message:
Implement arena-based region deallocation (Phase 1) (#65)

* Implement arena-based region deallocation (Phase 1)

Add SaveArenaState/RestoreArenaState IR instructions that snapshot and
restore the heap bump-allocator cursor at ownership scope boundaries.

When a scope's result is a copy type (Int, Float, Bool), RestoreArenaState
resets the heap cursor to the scope-entry watermark after Drop instructions
have run, effectively freeing all heap memory allocated within the scope.

Changes:
- Ir.cs: Add SaveArenaState and RestoreArenaState IR instruction records
- Lowering.cs: Emit SaveArenaState at PushOwnershipScope, conditionally
  emit RestoreArenaState at PopOwnershipScope for copy-type results
- LlvmCodegen.cs: Dispatch new instructions to backend codegen
- LlvmCodegenMemory.cs: Implement EmitSaveArenaState/EmitRestoreArenaState
- ArenaDeallocationTests.cs: 16 new tests covering IR emission, slot
  pairing, copy vs heap type behavior, nesting, and Drop interaction
- Update snapshot verified files for new IR shape
- Update COMPILER_OPTIMIZATION.md to document Phase 1 completion

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/bb46feac-16f7-4c4e-b2ab-ad45abb143e0

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix arena watermark placement: emit SaveArenaState before let-bound value lowering

Decouples SaveArenaState emission from PushOwnershipScope into a separate
EmitArenaWatermark() method. In LowerLet, the watermark is now emitted
BEFORE lowering let.Value so heap allocations from the bound expression
(lists, tuples, ADTs) are covered by the arena scope.

Adds 3 tests verifying SaveArenaState precedes heap Alloc instructions
for list and tuple let-bound values, and that copy-type bodies still
emit RestoreArenaState to reclaim those allocations.

* Fix match arm failure path: restore arena state on pattern/guard failure

Add per-arm cleanup label (Label → RestoreArenaState → Jump) so that when
a pattern or guard fails, the arena watermark is restored before jumping to
the next arm, preventing allocation accumulation from failed arms.

Update tests: strengthen cleanup path verification with structural assertions
(Label/Restore/Jump sequence), update string-result match test to account for
cleanup path RestoreArenaState.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Chapter 13: Object Graph Validation

Inventory how each validation invocation model handles complex object
graphs with recursive walks and how validation results are represented:

- Core Validator.TryValidateObject: single-level only, no recursion
- ASP.NET Core MVC: full recursive graph walk via ValidationVisitor
  with cycle detection and configurable MaxValidationDepth
- Blazor: top-level only by default; experimental ObjectGraphDataAnnotationsValidator
- Options validation: opt-in recursion via [ValidateObjectMembers]
  and [ValidateEnumeratedItems] attributes
- Microsoft.Extensions.Validation (.NET 10): full recursive graph walk
- EF Core and OpenAPI: metadata only, no runtime validation

Ecosystem projects that fill the recursion gap:
- MiniValidation (Damian Edwards): recursive with cycle detection
- RecursiveDataAnnotationsValidation, DataAnnotationsValidatorRecursive
- FluentValidation: child validators and RuleForEach

Includes comparison table and implications for async validation design.
Updates nav links on ch12, appendix-a, and README TOC.
